verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a well-characterized lysosomal ABC transporter. Multiple independent studies using immunofluorescence, subcellular fractionation, co-localization with LAMP-1/2, and RUSH trafficking assays unambiguously place full-length ABCB9 in the lysosomal limiting membrane, where it transports polypeptides from the cytosol into the lysosomal lumen. Lysosomal targeting is entirely encoded by the N-terminal TMD0 domain; the protein takes a direct ER→Golgi→endosome→lysosome route. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/stress/cell-death/activation outer-leaflet ecto-pool for this protein; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — ABCB9 is expressed in Sertoli cells and dendritic cells but always in the lysosomal membrane, never the plasma membrane outer leaflet; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — no evidence of lysosomal exocytosis-mediated surface exposure; (4) dual_localization — the coreTAPL truncation mutant transiently traffics to PM before degradation, but full-length ABCB9 does not; no documented PM pool for the full-length protein; (5) stable_surface_attachment — ABCB9 is a multi-pass TM lysosomal protein, not a secreted protein tethered to a surface partner. Verdict: endomembrane_resident.
